name: Native Pi agent vs pi-subagents actual evaluation

description: Fresh native agent vs pi-subagents actual eval baseline

asi.hypothesis: A fresh, isolated eval using source-backed evidence plus cheap tmux captures will show current native Pi has closed much of the old delegation/status/diagnostics gap while pi-subagents keeps async/control but has removed requested manager/status slash surfaces.

isolation_proof.native: Launched with --no-session --no-extensions --tools agent,read,grep,find,ls --thinking off; native captures used /agents, /agents-doctor, /agents-status only; no subagent tool/extension commands used.
isolation_proof.pi_subagents: Launched with --no-session --no-builtin-tools --no-extensions -e pi-subagents/src/extension/index.ts --thinking off; native built-in agent disabled; extension captures avoided /agents and native agent.

asi.task_agent_coverage: S09 source probe found no native action/taskId/create/list/get/update/delete lifecycle schema in packages/coding-agent/src/core/tools/agent.ts; pi-subagents management actions are not a general task-list equivalent.
key_findings: Native now has /agents-doctor, /agents-status, saved chains, and /agents run/parallel/run-chain scaffolds.
Installed pi-subagents is 0.24.0 and has removed /subagents manager UI and /subagents-status slash overlay.
Removed /subagents and /subagents-status probes fell through to parent model turns invoking extension subagent list, costing roughly 22k prompt and 187 completion tokens total.
No live child-agent outputs were run; source evidence used to avoid paid child runs.
asi.next_action_hint: If the next question is real output quality/token cost, run one symmetric low-cost live child scenario for S01-S03 under the same launch flags; otherwise prioritize implementing/verifying the native non-spawn task lifecycle surface for S09.
limitations: No live child-agent report quality measured.
Exact cache creation/read token accounting unavailable.
tmux captures prove command/UI surfaces but not deep selector semantics.

description: Add evidence manifest and integrity scoring

asi.hypothesis: The previous actual_eval_score was saturated on artifact presence; adding a real evidence manifest and scorer checks for scorecard evidence-file integrity should improve actual evaluation quality without padding or extra paid child-agent runs.
evidence: evidence-manifest.md maps all 18 scorecard rows to concrete evidence files and supporting live/source captures
./autoresearch.sh verifies every scorecard evidence file exists, manifest row coverage is complete, live capture links are present, and pi-subagents 0.24.0 removed-surface guards remain
run_experiment parsed actual_eval_score=307, evidence_file_coverage=18, evidence_manifest_rows=18, live_capture_links=8, version_guard_verified=1

isolation_proof.native: No native-arm scenario was rerun or changed to use pi-subagents; existing launch proof remains --no-extensions with native agent/read/grep/find/ls only.
isolation_proof.pi_subagents: No extension-arm scenario used native /agents or native agent; existing launch proof remains --no-builtin-tools --no-extensions -e pi-subagents.

asi.why_not_overfit: The new scorer dimension catches stale or missing evidence paths and removed-version drift; it would fail if scorecard rows cited nonexistent captures, so it rewards reproducibility rather than keyword padding.
asi.next_action_hint: Next most valuable iteration is either a single symmetric low-cost live child run for S01/S02 output quality, or rerun S09 if native task lifecycle code lands; avoid repeating removed-command fallthrough probes unless measuring UX cost.
limitations: No new child-agent model run; this iteration improves evidence integrity, not live output quality.
Metric formula changed within the same actual_eval_score goal to remove saturation; compare against prior score with that caveat.
